sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 45-year-old male, presented with an ankle injury sustained while playing basketball with his son.
He described an awkward landing and twisting of his ankle, resulting in pain and swelling on the outside of his ankle, which is visible and bruised.
He reports difficulty walking and feeling instability in his ankle.
He had recently been working out regularly and was concerned about disrupting his routine.
His son, who is 10 years old, is a fan of Steph Curry and enjoys playing basketball, but the patient is concerned about the high-impact nature of the sport for his son.
He is a volunteer coach and will start coaching in April.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's ankle is swollen on the lateral side, with exquisite tenderness to palpation, particularly on the lateral and medial deltoid regions.
The skin is intact, and capillary refill is brisk.
There is no malalignment.
Test Results: An X-ray of the right ankle revealed a displaced lateral malleolus fracture at the Weber C level, with no evidence of medial or posterior malleolar fractures.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral malleolar fracture that is currently unstable and requires surgical intervention.
Problem list: Lateral malleolar fracture; suspected instability; confirmed ankle injury.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg orally twice a day for pain and inflammation.
I instructed the patient to ice the ankle and elevate it regularly, particularly while working from home.
I also recommended using crutches to avoid further injury.
I advised the patient to continue coaching his son in April and to return to exercise within three months.
I will monitor the patient's progress and adjust the treatment plan as necessary.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment in three months to assess the healing of the fracture and progress with rehabilitation.
